channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel invades Lebanon, displacing more than a million residents
item.description: As the Iran war enters its second month, Israel has invaded Lebanon to drive out Iran-backed Hezbollah and a humanitarian disaster is unfolding as over one million Lebanese people are displaced.
item.content:encoded: As the Iran war enters its second month, Israel has invaded Lebanon to drive out Iran-backed Hezbollah and a humanitarian disaster is unfolding as over one million Lebanese people are displaced.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Former national security advisor discusses the ongoing talks between the US and Iran
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Biden administration National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan for his reaction to the latest developments in the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks Biden administration National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan for his reaction to the latest developments in the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: How much protein do you need? Here's how to personalize your optimal intake
item.description: The Dietary Guidelines released this year recommend higher levels of this essential nutrient. But protein needs are personal. Here's how to assess yours.
item.content:encoded: The Dietary Guidelines released this year recommend higher levels of this essential nutrient. But protein needs are personal. Here's how to assess yours.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Over one million Lebanese displaced by Israel's invasion, thousands of U.S. troops deployed to the Middle East, with more on the way, delays continue at U.S. airports with no funding deal in sight.
item.content:encoded: Over one million Lebanese displaced by Israel's invasion, thousands of U.S. troops deployed to the Middle East, with more on the way, delays continue at U.S. airports with no funding deal in sight.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Expert talks about the Pentagon's use of artificial intelligence
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Tristan Harris, co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, about the use of AI by the Pentagon.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Tristan Harris, co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, about the use of AI by the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Catholic bishops back birthright citizenship ahead of SCOTUS decision
item.description: Ahead of a Supreme Court case, U.S. Catholic bishops have filed a brief in support of birthright citizenship, arguing that its absence would "increase the susceptibility of children to statelessness."
item.content:encoded: Ahead of a Supreme Court case, U.S. Catholic bishops have filed a brief in support of birthright citizenship, arguing that its absence would "increase the susceptibility of children to statelessness."
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Emergency calls reveal conditions at the nation's largest immigration detention camp
item.description: In El Paso, Texas, 911 calls reveal conditions in the nation's largest immigration detention camp.
item.content:encoded: In El Paso, Texas, 911 calls reveal conditions in the nation's largest immigration detention camp.
item.dc:creator: Angela Kocherga

item.title: The Final Four is set; UConn joins Illinois, Arizona and Michigan
item.description: After UConn's stunning comeback, the Final Four is set in the NCAA men's basketball tournament: the Huskies join Arizona, Michigan and Illinois.
item.content:encoded: After UConn's stunning comeback, the Final Four is set in the NCAA men's basketball tournament: the Huskies join Arizona, Michigan and Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
item.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.
item.content:encoded:
Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

(Image credit: Arterra)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Thousands of US troops sent to the Middle East with more on the way
item.description: Additional U.S. troops have reached the Middle East, with more on the way. While the U.S. military hasn't specified a mission, the critical Strait of Hormuz remains closed to almost all oil tankers.
item.content:encoded: Additional U.S. troops have reached the Middle East, with more on the way. While the U.S. military hasn't specified a mission, the critical Strait of Hormuz remains closed to almost all oil tankers.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre
